canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, UltraChat is most commonly characterized as a diverse, multi-turn conversational dataset used to operationalize and train for instruction following. The definitions consistently state it was generated by GPT-3.5-Turbo. Its prevailing application is in instruction fine-tuning, with several sources noting its use for this purpose, referring to it as a "large-scale dataset of instructional conversations" and a common "IFT dataset." A related framing, from one paper, positions it as a benchmark for "interactive dialogue." Beyond these primary uses, a smaller set of applications exists. For instance, one study employs UltraChat as a source of "benign" data to evaluate safety-patching procedures. Another paper reports its use for measuring commonsense knowledge, though this is a less frequent association in the provided data.
papers: Can Large Language Models Act as Ensembler for Multi-GNNs?

definitions.text: A diverse, multi-turn conversational instruction-following dataset generated by GPT-3.5-Turbo, used for supervised fine-tuning and for generating prompts for self-training.
definitions.paper: Better Instruction-Following Through Minimum Bayes Risk

relationships.paper: Model Equality Testing: Which Model is this API Serving?
relationships.evidence: We are interested in the task of instruction-following, for which we use UltraChat (Ding et al., 2023), a large-scale dataset of instructional conversations. (Section 6.1)
